source: oeis.org (The On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences)
license: OEIS content is CC-BY-NC 4.0; attribution required.

A000001.name: Number of groups of order n.

A000058.name: Sylvester's sequence: a(n+1) = a(n)^2 - a(n) + 1, with a(0) = 2.

A000372.name: Dedekind numbers or Dedekind's problem: number of monotone Boolean functions of n variables, number of antichains of subsets of an n-set, number of elements in a free distributive lattice on n generat
A000372.formula: The asymptotics can be found in the Korshunov paper. - _Boris Bukh_, Nov 07 2003

A000445.name: Latest possible occurrence of the first consecutive pair of n-th power residues, modulo any prime.

A001009.name: Triangle giving number L(n,k) of normalized k X n Latin rectangles.

A001034.name: Orders of noncyclic simple groups (without repetition).

A001220.name: Wieferich primes: primes p such that p^2 divides 2^(p-1) - 1.
A001220.formula: (A178815(A000720(p))^(p-1) - 1) mod p^2 = A178900(n), where p = a(n). - _Jonathan Sondow_, Jun 29 2010

A001223.name: Prime gaps: differences between consecutive primes.
A001223.formula: G.f.: b(x)*(1-x), where b(x) is the g.f. for the primes. - _Franklin T. Adams-Watters_, Jun 15 2006

A001274.name: Numbers k such that phi(k) = phi(k+1).
A001274.formula: Conjecture: a(n) ~ C*n^3*log(n), where C = 9/Pi^2 = 0.91189... - _Thomas Ordowski_, Oct 21 2014

A001438.name: Maximal number of mutually orthogonal Latin squares (or MOLS) of order n.
A001438.formula: a(n) <= n-1 for all n>1. - _Tom Edgar_, Apr 27 2015

A001661.name: Largest number not the sum of distinct positive n-th powers.

A001694.name: Powerful numbers, definition (1): if a prime p divides n then p^2 must also divide n (also called squareful, square full, square-full or 2-powerful numbers).

A002048.name: Segmented numbers, or prime numbers of measurement.
A002048.formula: Andrews conjectures that lim_{n -> oo} n log n / (a(n) loglog n) = 1. - _N. J. A. Sloane_, Dec 01 2013

A002182.name: Highly composite numbers: numbers n where d(n), the number of divisors of n (A000005), increases to a record.
A002182.formula: Also, for n >= 2, smallest values of p for which A006218(p)-A006318(p-1) = A002183(n). - Philippe LALLOUET (philip.lallouet(AT)wanadoo.fr), Jun 23 2007

A002202.name: Values taken by totient function phi(m) (A000010).

A002233.name: a(1) = 1; for n > 1, a(n) = least positive prime primitive root of n-th prime.
A002233.formula: a(n) = A122028(n) for n>1. - _Jonathan Sondow_, May 18 2017

A002386.name: Primes (lower end) with record gaps to the next consecutive prime: primes p(k) where p(k+1) - p(k) exceeds p(j+1) - p(j) for all j < k.

A002503.name: Numbers k such that binomial(2*k,k) is divisible by (k+1)^2.

A002583.name: Largest prime factor of n! + 1.
A002583.formula: Erdős & Stewart show that a(n) > n + (1-o(1))log n/log log n and lim sup a(n)/n > 2. - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, Dec 05 2012

A002827.name: Unitary perfect numbers: numbers k such that usigma(k) - k = k.
A002827.formula: If m is a term and omega(m) = A001221(m) = k, then m < 2^(2^k) (Goto, 2007). - _Amiram Eldar_, Jun 06 2020

A002858.name: Ulam numbers: a(1) = 1; a(2) = 2; for n>2, a(n) = least number > a(n-1) which is a unique sum of two distinct earlier terms.

A002975.name: Primitive weird numbers: weird numbers with no proper weird divisors.

A003002.name: Size of the largest subset of the numbers [1...n] which does not contain a 3-term arithmetic progression.
A003002.formula: Sanders proves that a(n) << n*(log log n)^5/log n. - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, Jan 22 2016

A003003.name: Size of the largest subset of the numbers [1...n] which doesn't contain a 4-term arithmetic progression.

A003004.name: Size of the largest subset of the numbers [1..n] which does not contain a 5-term arithmetic progression.

A003005.name: Size of the largest subset of the numbers [1..n] which doesn't contain a 6-term arithmetic progression.

A003015.name: Numbers that occur 5 or more times in Pascal's triangle.

A003016.name: Number of occurrences of n as an entry in rows <= n of Pascal's triangle (A007318).

A003022.name: Length of shortest (or optimal) Golomb ruler with n marks.
A003022.formula: a(n) >= n(n-1)/2, with strict inequality for n >= 5 (Golomb). - _David W. Wilson_, Aug 18 2007

A003034.name: Sylvester's problem: minimal number of ordinary lines through n points in the plane.
A003034.formula: Kelly and Moser showed that a(n) >= ceiling(3n/7); Csima and Sawyer showed that a(n) >= floor(6n/13) except for n=7.

A003035.name: Maximal number of 3-tree rows in n-tree orchard problem.

A003135.name: n! is a nontrivial product of factorials. It is conjectured that the list is complete.

A003142.name: Largest subset of 3 X 3 X ... X 3 cube (in n dimensions) with no 3 points collinear.

A003323.name: Multicolor Ramsey numbers R(3,3,...,3), where there are n 3's.
A003323.formula: The limit of a(n)^(1/n) exists and is at least 3.28 (possibly infinite). (See the survey by Radziszowski.) - _Pontus von Brömssen_, Jul 23 2021 (updated Mar 13 2025)

A003458.name: Erdős-Selfridge function: a(n) is the least number m > n+1 such that the least prime factor of binomial(m, n) is > n.

A003829.name: Maximal number of unit circles through n points in plane, each circle containing 3 of the points.

A004059.name: a(n) gives position of first n in A057561.

A004539.name: Expansion of sqrt(2) in base 2.
A004539.formula: a(k) = floor(Sum_{n>=1} A005875(n)/exp(Pi*n/(2^((2/3)*k+(1/3))))) mod 2. Will give the k-th binary digit of sqrt(2). A005875 : number of ways to write n as sum of 3 squares. - _Simon Plouffe_, Dec 30

A005113.name: Smallest prime in class n (sometimes written n+) according to the Erdős-Selfridge classification of primes.

A005115.name: Let i, i+d, i+2d, ..., i+(n-1)d be an n-term arithmetic progression of primes; choose the one which minimizes the last term; then a(n) = last term i+(n-1)d.
A005115.formula: Green & Tao prove that this sequence is infinite, and further a(n) < 2^2^2^2^2^2^2^2^O(n). Granville conjectures that a(n) <= n! + 1 for n >= 3 and give a heuristic suggesting a(n) is around (exp(1-ga

A005117.name: Squarefree numbers: numbers that are not divisible by a square greater than 1.
A005117.formula: Limit_{n->oo} a(n)/n = Pi^2/6 (see A013661). - _Benoit Cloitre_, May 23 2002

A005153.name: Practical numbers: positive integers m such that every k <= sigma(m) is a sum of distinct divisors of m. Also called panarithmic numbers.
A005153.formula: Weingartner proves that a(n) ~ k*n log n, strengthening an earlier result of Saias. In particular, a(n) = k*n log n + O(n log log n). - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, May 10 2013

A005185.name: Hofstadter Q-sequence: a(1) = a(2) = 1; a(n) = a(n-a(n-1)) + a(n-a(n-2)) for n > 2.

A005235.name: Fortunate numbers: least m > 1 such that m + prime(n)# is prime, where p# denotes the product of all primes <= p.
A005235.formula: If x(n) = 1 + Product_{i=1..n} prime(i), q(n) = least prime > x(n), then a(n) = q(n) - x(n) + 1.

A005236.name: Barriers for omega(n): numbers n such that, for all m < n, m + omega(m) <= n.

A005237.name: Numbers k such that k and k+1 have the same number of divisors.

A005243.name: A self-generating sequence: start with 1 and 2, take all sums of any number of successive previous elements and adjoin them to the sequence. Repeat!

A005244.name: A self-generating sequence: start with 2 and 3, take all products of any 2 previous elements, subtract 1 and adjoin them to the sequence.

A005278.name: Noncototients: numbers k such that x - phi(x) = k has no solution.

A005279.name: Numbers having divisors d, e with d < e < 2*d.

A005282.name: Mian-Chowla sequence (a B_2 sequence): a(1) = 1; for n>1, a(n) = smallest number > a(n-1) such that the pairwise sums of elements are all distinct.

A005420.name: Largest prime factor of 2^n - 1.
A005420.formula: a(n) = a(2n) iff a(n) > A002587(n). See A337431. - _Thomas Ordowski_, Jan 07 2014

A005487.name: Starts 0, 4 and contains no 3-term arithmetic progression.

A006037.name: Weird numbers: abundant (A005101) but not pseudoperfect (A005835).

A006065.name: Maximal number of 4-tree rows in n-tree orchard problem.

A006285.name: Odd numbers not of form p + 2^k (de Polignac numbers).

A006370.name: The Collatz or 3x+1 map: a(n) = n/2 if n is even, 3n + 1 if n is odd.

A006517.name: Numbers k such that k divides 2^k + 2.

A006521.name: Numbers n such that n divides 2^n + 1.

A006530.name: Gpf(n): greatest prime dividing n, for n >= 2; a(1)=1.

A006560.name: Smallest starting prime for n consecutive primes in arithmetic progression.

A006585.name: Egyptian fractions: number of solutions to 1 = 1/x_1 + ... + 1/x_n in positive integers x_1 < ... < x_n.

A006672.name: a(n) = smallest m such that for every red-blue edge-coloring of the graph K_{m} there exists either a red 4-cycle or a blue K_{1,n}; Ramsey number r(C_4, K_{1,n}).

A006855.name: Maximum number of edges in an n-node squarefree graph, or, in a graph containing no 4-cycle, or no C_4.
A006855.formula: a(n) <= n^(3/2)*(1/2 + o(1)) [Kovari, Sos, Turan]. But the upper bound mentioned in the Aigner-Ziegler reference (see above) is stronger. - _N. J. A. Sloane_, Mar 07 2022

A006856.name: Maximal number of edges in n-node graph of girth at least 5.

A006931.name: Least Carmichael number with n prime factors, or 0 if no such number exists.

A007539.name: a(n) = first n-fold perfect (or n-multiperfect) number.

A007865.name: Number of sum-free subsets of {1, ..., n}.

A008407.name: Minimal difference s(n) between beginning and end of n consecutive large primes (n-tuplet) permitted by divisibility considerations.
A008407.formula: s(k), k >= 2, is smallest s such that there exist B = {b_1, b_2, ..., b_k} with s = b_k - b_1 and such that for all primes p <= k, not all residues modulo p are represented by B.

A008908.name: a(n) = (1 + number of halving and tripling steps to reach 1 in the Collatz (3x+1) problem), or -1 if 1 is never reached.

A008997.name: Orchard problem with 5 trees in a row (may not have all been proved optimal).

A014197.name: Number of numbers m with Euler phi(m) = n.
A014197.formula: Dirichlet g.f.: Sum_{n>=1} a(n)*n^-s = zeta(s)*Product_(1+1/(p-1)^s-1/p^s). - _Benoit Cloitre_, Apr 12 2003

A014544.name: Numbers k such that a cube can be divided into k subcubes.

A015919.name: Positive integers k such that 2^k == 2 (mod k).
A015919.formula: Equals {1} U A000040 U A001567 U A006935 = A001567 U A006935 U A008578. - _Ray Chandler_, Dec 07 2003; corrected by _Max Alekseyev_, Feb 11 2015

A015921.name: Positive integers n such that 2^n == 4 (mod n).

A015940.name: Positive integers n such that 2^n == -3 (mod n).

A023193.name: a(n) gives the largest number k for which there is at least one admissible k-tuple taken from [0, 1, ..., n-1] if the tuple starts with 0. Admissibility is defined in a comment.
A023193.formula: Conjecturally, a(n) = lim sup pi(x+n)-pi(x), where pi = A000720. This would follow from the k-tuple conjecture. - _David W. Wilson_, May 23 2005

A025418.name: Least sum of 3 positive cubes in exactly n ways.
A025418.formula: {min k: A025456(k) = n}. - _R. J. Mathar_, Jun 15 2018

A025456.name: Number of partitions of n into 3 positive cubes.
A025456.formula: a(n) = [x^n y^3] Product_{k>=1} 1/(1 - y*x^(k^3)). - _Ilya Gutkovskiy_, Apr 23 2019

A025494.name: Squares which are the sum of factorials of distinct integers (probably finite).

A027434.name: a(1) = 2; then defined by property that a(n) = smallest number >= a(n-1) such that successive runs have lengths 1,1,2,2,3,3,4,4.

A027627.name: Maximal cardinality of 2-distance set in R^n.

A028391.name: a(n) = n - floor(sqrt(n)).
A028391.formula: a(n) = ceiling(n - sqrt(n)), as follows from ceiling(-x) = -floor(x). [Corrected by _M. F. Hasler_, Feb 21 2010]

A030659.name: Smallest possible maximum denominator in an expression for 1 as a sum of n distinct unit (Egyptian) fractions.

A030979.name: Numbers k such that binomial(2k,k) is not divisible by 3, 5 or 7.
A030979.formula: Intersection of A005836, A037453 and A037461. - _T. D. Noe_, Apr 18 2007

A032742.name: a(1) = 1; for n > 1, a(n) = largest proper divisor of n (that is, for n>1, maximum divisor d of n in range 1 <= d < n).

A034258.name: Write n! as a product of n numbers, n! = k(1)*k(2)*...*k(n) with k(1) <= k(2) <= ..., in all possible ways; a(n) = max value of k(1).
A034258.formula: If p is prime, a(p-1) = a(p). - _Bernard Schott_, Oct 24 2021

A034259.name: Smallest m such that A034258(m) >= n.

A034463.name: Maximal number of residue classes mod n such that no subset adds to 0.

A036236.name: Least inverse of A015910: smallest integer k > 0 such that 2^k mod k = n, or 0 if no such k exists.
A036236.formula: It's obvious that for each k, a(k) > k and we can easily prove that 2^(3^n) = 3^n-1 (mod 3^n). So 3^n is the least k with 2^k mod k = 3^n-1. Hence for each n, a(3^n-1) = 3^n. - _Farideh Firoozbakht_,

A038133.name: From a subtractive Goldbach conjecture: odd primes that are not cluster primes.

A038372.name: Largest subset of integers [ 1...n ] such that no member divides two others.
A038372.formula: a(n) >= ceiling(2n/3). For large n, 0.6725n <= a(n) <= 0.6736n (see Guy).

A039651.name: Number of iterations of f(x) = phi(x)+1 on n required to reach a prime.

A039669.name: Numbers n > 2 such that n - 2^k is a prime for all k > 0 with 2^k < n.

A045535.name: Least negative pseudosquare modulo the first n odd primes.

A046693.name: Size of smallest subset S of N={0,1,2,...,n} such that S-S=N, where S-S={abs(i-j) | i,j in S}.

A048669.name: The Jacobsthal function g(n): maximal gap in a list of all the integers relatively prime to n.

A048670.name: Jacobsthal function A048669 applied to the product of the first n primes (A002110).

A048892.name: Start of n consecutive integers with distinct number of divisors.

A049108.name: a(n) is the number of iterations of Euler phi function needed to reach 1 starting at n (n is counted).
A049108.formula: By the definition of a(n) we have for n >= 2 the recursion a(n) = a(Phi(n)) + 1. - Ahmed Fares (ahmedfares(AT)my-deja.com), Apr 20 2001

A051487.name: Numbers k such that phi(k) = phi(k - phi(k)).

A051488.name: Numbers k such that phi(k) < phi(k - phi(k)).

A051761.name: Numbers that are simultaneously a sum of factorials of distinct integers and of the form a^b with b >= 2.

A053597.name: Let prime(i) = i-th prime (A000040), let d(i) = prime(i+1)-prime(i) (A001223); a(n) = number of distinct numbers among d(n), d(n+1), d(n+2), ... before first duplicate is encountered.

A053760.name: Smallest positive quadratic nonresidue modulo p, where p is the n-th prime.
A053760.formula: a(n) = A020649(prime(n)) for n > 1. - _Thomas Ordowski_, Apr 24 2019

A054377.name: Primary pseudoperfect numbers: numbers k > 1 such that 1/k + sum 1/p = 1, where the sum is over the primes p | k.

A055265.name: a(n) is the smallest positive integer not already in the sequence such that a(n)+a(n-1) is prime, starting with a(1)=1.

A056604.name: a(0)=1; thereafter a(n) = lcm(1, 2, 3, 4, ..., prime(n)).
A056604.formula: a(n) = prime(n)^r(n) *...* prime(1)^r(1) for maximal prime(j)^r(j) <= prime(n).

A056828.name: Numbers that are not the sum of at most three powerful numbers (A001694).

A057521.name: Powerful part of n: if n = Product_i (pi^ei) then a(n) = Product_{i : ei > 1} (pi^ei); if n = b*c^2*d^3 then a(n) = c^2*d^3 when b is minimized.

A057561.name: Size of the largest set encompassing no {x, 2x, 3x} contained in D(n) = the first n 3-smooth numbers {1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 16, 18, 24, 27, ...} (A003586).

A058989.name: Largest number of consecutive integers such that each is divisible by a prime <= the n-th prime.
A058989.formula: a(n) = A048670(n) - 1. See that entry for additional information.

A059233.name: Number of rows in which n appears in Pascal's triangle A007318.
A059233.formula: a(A180058(n)) = n and a(m) < n for m < A180058(n); a(A182237(n)) = 2; a(A098565(n)) = 3. - _Reinhard Zumkeller_, Dec 24 2012

A059442.name: Array of Ramsey numbers R(n,k) (n >= 2, k >= 2) read by antidiagonals.

A060355.name: Numbers k such that k and k+1 are powerful numbers.

A060407.name: Maximal number of pairwise edge-disjoint monochromatic K_3's in a K_n for any 2-coloring of the edges of K_n.

A060427.name: Smallest prime p such that there are n strings of consecutive integers all having products = 1 mod p.

A060859.name: Powerful numbers of the form k^2 - 1.
A060859.formula: a(n) = k^2 - 1 and a(n) + 1 = k^2 are consecutive powerful numbers.

A061070.name: Number of distinct values in the list of values of the Euler totient function {phi(j) : j=1..n}.

A061092.name: a(0) = 1; for n>0, a(n) = smallest prime of the form k*a(n-1) + 1.

A062241.name: Smallest integer >= 2 that is not the sum of 2 positive integers whose prime factors are all <= prime(n), the n-th prime.

A062249.name: a(n) = n + d(n), where d(n) = number of divisors of n, cf. A000005.
A062249.formula: a(n) = n + A000005(n). - _Omar E. Pol_, Dec 12 2008

A063980.name: Pillai primes: primes p such that there exists an integer m such that m! + 1 == 0 (mod p) and p != 1 (mod m).

A064113.name: Indices k such that (1/3)*(prime(k)+prime(k+1)+prime(k+2)) is a prime.

A064152.name: Erdős primes: primes p such that all p-k! for 1 <= k! < p are composite.

A064164.name: EHS numbers: k such that there is a prime p satisfying k! + 1 == 0 (mod p) and p !== 1 (mod k).

A064491.name: a(1) = 1, a(n+1) = a(n) + tau(a(n)), where tau(n) (A000005) is the number of divisors of n.
A064491.formula: It seems likely that there exist constants c_1 and c_2 such that c_1*n*log(n) < a(n) < c_2*n*log(n) for all sufficiently large n. - _Franklin T. Adams-Watters_, Jun 25 2008

A066063.name: Size of the smallest subset S of T={0,1,2,...,n} such that each element of T is the sum of two elements of S.

A066766.name: Decimal expansion of Sum_{k>=1} sigma(k)/2^k where sigma(k) is the sum of divisors of k, 1 <= d <= k.

A067720.name: Numbers k such that phi(k^2+1) = k*phi(k+1).

A068063.name: Maximum cardinality of a nondividing subset of {1, 2, ..., n}.

A068509.name: a(n) = maximum length of a subset in {1,..,n} whose integers have pairwise LCM not exceeding n.
A068509.formula: (3*sqrt(n))/(2*sqrt(2)) - 2 < a(n) <= 1.638*sqrt(n). - P. Erdos and S. L. G. Choi

A070003.name: Numbers divisible by the square of their largest prime factor.
A070003.formula: Erdős proved that there are x * exp(-(1 + o(1))sqrt(log x log log x)) members of this sequence up to x. - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, Mar 26 2012

A070089.name: P(n) < P(n+1) where P(n) (A006530) is the largest prime factor of n.

A071626.name: Number of distinct exponents in the prime factorization of n!.

A071799.name: Number of lattice paths in the lattice [0..2n] X [0..2n] which do not pass through the point (n,n).

A071870.name: Numbers k such that gpf(k) > gpf(k+1) > gpf(k+2) where gpf(k) denotes the largest prime factor of k.

A072207.name: a(0) = 1; for n>0, a(n) = number of distinct sums of subsets of {1, 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, ..., 1/n} (allowing the empty subset).
A072207.formula: a(p) = 2 * a(p-1) for p prime. a(2*p) = 2 * a(2*p-1) for p>3 prime. - _Giovanni Resta_, Jul 18 2019

A072937.name: Least k such that prime(n) appears in factorization of k! + 1.

A073101.name: Number of integer solutions (x,y,z) to 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z satisfying 0 < x < y < z.

A073944.name: a(n) is the smallest m such that n-th prime divides m! + 1.

A074399.name: a(n) is the largest prime divisor of n(n+1).

A074741.name: Sum of squares of gaps between consecutive primes.

A074781.name: Primes of the form p*2^k + 1 for any k and any prime p.

A075245.name: x-value of the solution (x,y,z) to 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z satisfying 0 < x < y < z and having the largest z-value. The y and z components are in A075246 and A075247.
A075245.formula: Conjecture: a(n) = floor(n/4) + d, with d = 1 except for some n = 8k+1 (k = 6, 9, 11, 14, 20, 21, 24, 29, 30, 35, 39, ...) where d = 2 . - _M. F. Hasler_, Jul 02 2022

A075246.name: y-value of the solution (x,y,z) to 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z satisfying 0 < x < y < z and having the largest z-value. The x and z components are in A075245 and A075247.

A075247.name: Largest possible z-value of an integer solution (x,y,z) to 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z satisfying 0 < x < y < z. The x and y components are in A075245 and A075246.

A075248.name: Number of solutions (x,y,z) to 5/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z satisfying 0 < x < y < z.

A076259.name: Gaps between squarefree numbers: a(n) = A005117(n+1) - A005117(n).
A076259.formula: Asymptotic mean: lim_{n->oo} (1/n) Sum_{k=1..n} a(k) = Pi^2/6 (A013661). - _Amiram Eldar_, Oct 21 2020

A076336.name: (Provable) Sierpiński numbers: odd numbers n such that for all k >= 1 the numbers n*2^k + 1 are composite.

A076393.name: Decimal expansion of Vardi constant arising in the Sylvester sequence.

A076445.name: The smaller of a pair of powerful numbers (A001694) that differ by 2.

A076871.name: Sum of two powerful numbers (definition (1), A001694).

A076872.name: a(n) = number of numbers <= n that are the sum of two squarefull numbers.

A078515.name: Numbers n such that A053597(n) sets a new record.

A080200.name: Numbers that do not occur as differences between terms of the Mian-Chowla sequence A005282.

A083550.name: Product of 2 consecutive prime differences of two successive terms of A001223.

A087280.name: Solutions n of max(m+d(m))=n+2 for m<n; d(m) is the number of divisors of m.

A087719.name: Least number m such that the number of numbers k <= m with k > spf(k)^n exceeds the number of numbers with k <= spf(k)^n.

A091963.name: a(n) is the smallest gcd of two interior numbers on row n of Pascal's triangle ("interior" means that the 1's at the ends of the rows are excluded).

A092487.name: a(n) = least k such that {n+1, n+2, n+3, ... n+k} has a subset the product of whose members with n is a square.

A092670.name: a(n) = number of Egyptian fractions 1 = 1/x_1 + ... + 1/x_k (for any k), 0<x_1<...<x_k<=n.

A092671.name: Numbers n such that there exists a solution to the equation 1 = 1/x_1 + ... + 1/x_k (for any k), 0 < x_1 < ... < x_k = n.

A094708.name: Size of the smallest set hitting all {x, 2x, 3x} contained in D(n) = the first n 3-smooth numbers {1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 16, 18, 24, 27,...} (A003586).

A097847.name: Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) = minimal number of terms needed to write k/n (for 1 <= k <= n) as a sum of unit fractions.
A097847.formula: T(n, n-1) = A330808(n). - _Jon E. Schoenfield_, Jan 11 2020

A097849.name: Maximal entry in row n of A097847.

A098565.name: Numbers that appear as binomial coefficients exactly 6 times.

A103309.name: Smallest prime primitive root of n that is less than n, or 0 if none exists.

A105206.name: Number of edges in a pancyclic graph on n+2 vertices with the fewest possible edges.

A109925.name: Number of primes of the form n - 2^k.

A110177.name: Number of solutions 0<k<n to the equation sigma(n) = sigma(k) + sigma(n-k), where sigma is the sum of divisors function.

A110566.name: a(n) = lcm{1,2,...,n}/denominator of harmonic number H(n).

A113827.name: Initial terms associated with the arithmetic progressions of primes in A005115.

A115645.name: Powerful(1) numbers (A001694) that are sums of distinct factorials.

A116446.name: Let Sq(n) denote the square grid consisting of all lattice points (x,y) such that x,y are in {0,1,...,n}. a(n) is the minimum number t such that there are t of the (n+1)^2 lattice points in Sq(n) so t

A119425.name: Primitive terms of the sequence A119357, i.e., of the sequence of those values of n for which the number of distinct nonzero sums of distinct divisors of n is less than 2^tau(n) - 1.

A121048.name: a(n) = n + phi(n), where phi is the Euler totient function.

A121359.name: Greatest prime factor of pyramidal number A000292(n).

A123556.name: Number of elements in longest possible arithmetic progression of primes with difference n.
A123556.formula: Assume the k-tuples conjecture. Let p = A053669(n). If the arithmetic progression of p elements starting at p with difference n consists of primes, then a(n) = p, otherwise a(n) = p-1.

A129515.name: Numbers m such that binomial(2*m, m) has the same prime factors as binomial(2*k, k) for some k > m.

A131628.name: Maximal size of an n-distance set in the plane.

A135311.name: A greedy sequence of prime offsets.

A137245.name: Decimal expansion of Sum_{p prime} 1/(p * log p).

A140462.name: Turan's upper bound on the number of triangles of a simplicial complex of dimension two for which every minimal non-face has three vertices.

A141399.name: Positive integers k such that the distinct primes that divide k or k+1 form a set of consecutive primes. In other words, k is included if and only if k*(k+1) is contained in sequence A073491.

A143301.name: Decimal expansion of the Hall-Montgomery constant.

A143823.name: Number of subsets {x(1),x(2),...,x(k)} of {1,2,...,n} such that all differences |x(i)-x(j)| are distinct.
A143823.formula: a(n) = A169947(n-1) + n + 1 for n>=2. - _Nathaniel Johnston_, Nov 12 2010

A143824.name: Size of the largest subset {x(1),x(2),...,x(k)} of {1,2,...,n} with the property that all differences |x(i)-x(j)| are distinct.
A143824.formula: For n > 1, a(n) = A325678(n - 1) + 1. - _Gus Wiseman_, Jun 07 2019

A146968.name: Brocard's problem: positive integers n such that n!+1 = m^2.

A154554.name: Primes p such that m=p-1 is the least number such that p divides m!+1.

A155085.name: a(n) = n + sum of divisors of n.
A155085.formula: If n is a prime, a(n) = 2n+1.

A156816.name: Decimal expansion of the positive root of the equation 13x^4 - 7x^2 - 581 = 0.

A156989.name: Largest size of a subset of {1,2,3}^n that does not contain any combinatorial lines (i.e., strings formed by 1, 2, 3, and at least one instance of a wildcard x, with x then substituted for 1, 2, or 3,

A167485.name: Smallest positive integer m such that n can be expressed as the sum of an initial subsequence of the divisors of m, or 0 if no such m exists.

A171081.name: Van der Waerden numbers w(3, n).
A171081.formula: a(n) >= exp(c(log n)^(4/3) / (log log n)^(1/3)) for constant c > 0. (Green)

A175155.name: Numbers m satisfying m^2 + 1 = x^2 * y^3 for positive integers x and y.

A175769.name: Maximum cardinality of isosceles sets in E^n.

A180058.name: Smallest number occurring in exactly n rows of Pascal's triangle.

A181740.name: Number of sequences of length n over {1, -1} with Erdős discrepancy <= 2.

A182237.name: Numbers occurring exactly in 2 rows of Pascal's triangle.

A185661.name: Smallest set containing 1 and closed under the operations x->2x+1, x->3x+1, x->6x+1.

A186704.name: The minimum number of distinct distances determined by n points in the Euclidean plane.

A186705.name: The Erdős unit distance problem: the maximum number of occurrences of the same distance among n points in the plane.

A186736.name: Maximum sum of relatively prime integers no larger than n.

A192881.name: Number of terms for the shortest Egyptian fraction representation of 1 starting with 1/n.

A193429.name: a(n) = minimum value of the largest element of a nonempty set of positive integers > n such that their product is equal to n!, or 0 if no such set exists.

A194259.name: Number of distinct prime factors of p(1)*p(2)*...*p(n), where p(n) is the n-th partition number.

A194260.name: A194259(n) - n, where A194259(n) is the number of distinct prime factors of p(1)*p(2)*...*p(n) and p(n) is the n-th partition number.

A210184.name: Number of distinct residues of all factorials mod prime(n).

A213253.name: a(n) = smallest k such that highest prime factor of m(m+1)...(m+k-1) is > n if m > n.
A213253.formula: a(n) <= n (Sylvester's theorem--see Sylvester 1892, p. 4). - _Jonathan Sondow_, Jul 23 2013

A214583.name: Numbers m such that for all k with gcd(m, k) = 1 and m > k^2, m - k^2 is prime.

A217693.name: Numbers of distinct integers obtained from summing up subsets of {1, 1/2, 1/3, ..., 1/n}.

A219429.name: Highest prime primitive root (less than p) for the n-th prime p. (or 0 if none exists).

A226521.name: Triangle read by rows: T(n,k) = smallest prime == k (mod n) if gcd(k,n)=1, otherwise 0, for n >= 2, 1 <= k < n.

A228828.name: Numbers n such that n^2 + pi(n) is prime.

A229487.name: Conjectured greatest number that converges to prime(n) under the iteration x -> phi(x) + 1, where phi is Euler's totient function.

A231201.name: Number of ways to write n = x + y (x, y > 0) with 2^x + y prime.

A231255.name: a(n) is the smallest integer t such that every length-t walk from the origin (0,0) taking steps of either (0,1) or (1,0) is guaranteed to have n points that are collinear.
A231255.formula: A393935(n)-1 <= a(n) <= A380991(n-1) for n >= 2. - _Pontus von Brömssen_, May 05 2026

A232174.name: Number of ways to write n = x + y (x, y > 0) with x + n*y and x^2 + n*y^2 both prime.

A234813.name: Number of distinct integers of the form i+(i+1)+(i+2)+...+j, for 1 <= i <= j <= n.

A237695.name: Maximum length of a +- 1 sequence of discrepancy n.
A237695.formula: If a(n) exists for some positive n, then a(n) >= 9^(n-1). - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, Mar 03 2014

A239957.name: a(n) = |{0 < g < prime(n): g is a primitive root modulo prime(n) of the form k^2 + 1}|.

A245762.name: Maximal number of edges in a C_4 free subgraph of the n-cube.

A256435.name: First differences of sums of two squares.

A256519.name: Composites c for which an integer 1 < k < c exists such that (c-k)! == -1 (mod c).

A256936.name: Decimal expansion of Sum_{k>=1} phi(k)/2^k, where phi is Euler's totient function.

A262153.name: Decimal expansion of Sum_{p prime} 1/(2^p-1), a prime equivalent of the Erdős-Borwein constant.

A263647.name: Numbers k such that 2^k-1 and 3^k-1 are coprime.

A263958.name: Prime Riesel numbers p that are not Mersenne primes such that 2*p is a noncototient.

A263996.name: Smallest possible cardinality of the union of the set of pairwise sums and the set of pairwise products from a set of n positive integers.

A264810.name: Number of numbers k <= n such that phi(m) = k for some m.

A276661.name: Least k such that there is a set S in {1, 2, ..., k} with n elements and the property that each of its subsets has a distinct sum.
A276661.formula: a(n) <= A005318(n) [cf. Conway-Guy, Bohman]. - _Michael S. Branicky_, Jan 24 2026

A280831.name: Number of ways to write 8*n+7 as x^2 + y^2 + z^2 + w^2 with x^4 + 1680*y^3*z a square, where x,y,z,w are positive integers.

A280992.name: Squarefree triangular numbers that are products of consecutive primes.

A281976.name: Number of ways to write n as x^2 + y^2 + z^2 + w^2 with x,y,z,w nonnegative integers and z <= w such that both x and x + 24*y are squares.

A284783.name: Numbers k such that k and k + 5040 have the same number of divisors.

A287116.name: Nonsquare integers that cannot be represented in the form 4M-d, where (a*b)|M and d|(a+b) for some positive integers a,b.

A287616.name: Number of ways to write n as x*(x+1)/2 + y*(3*y+1)/2 + z*(5*z+1)/2 with x,y,z nonnegative integers.

A289280.name: a(n) is the least integer k > n such that any prime factor of k is also a prime factor of n.
A289280.formula: For any n > 1, n < a(n) <= n*lpf(n), where lpf = A020639.

A292528.name: Minimal number of vertices in a triangle-free graph with chromatic number n.
A292528.formula: For n > 3, the Mycielskian of a graph for a(n-1) shows that a(n) <= 2*a(n-1) + 1. This can be used to show that a(n) <= 3/4 * 2^n - 1 for n > 1.

A303656.name: Number of ways to write n as a^2 + b^2 + 3^c + 5^d, where a,b,c,d are nonnegative integers with a <= b.

A306477.name: Number of ways to write n as C(w+2,2) + C(x+3,4) + C(y+5,6) + C(z+7,8) with w,x,y,z nonnegative integers, where C(m,k) denotes the binomial coefficient m!/(k!*(m-k)!).

A308734.name: Number of ordered ways to write n as (2^a*3^b)^2 + (2^c*5^d)^2 + x^2 + y^2, where a,b,c,d,x,y are nonnegative integers with x <= y.

A322144.name: a(n) = Sum_{i=1..phi(n)-1} (r(i+1)-r(i))^2 where r(1) = 1 < ... < n-1 = r(phi(n)) are the phi(n) integers relatively prime to n.

A322292.name: a(n) = Max_{c composite, c < n} (c + least prime factor of c).

A325864.name: Number of subsets of {1..n} of which every subset has a different sum.

A327153.name: Number of divisors d of n such that sigma(d)*d is equal to n.
A327153.formula: a(n) = Sum_{d|n} [A000203(d)*d == n], where [ ] is the Iverson bracket.

A327909.name: a(n) is the smallest start of a run of n or more integers having a prime factor greater than n.

A330244.name: Weird numbers m (A006037) such that sigma(m)/m > sigma(k)/k for all weird numbers k < m, where sigma(m) is the sum of divisors of m (A000203).

A332077.name: Square array of sunflower numbers Sun(m,n) = minimal number of distinct sets of cardinality <= m such that there is a sunflower with at least n sets among them, read by falling antidiagonals; m, n >=
A332077.formula: Sun(m,n) = n for n <= 2 and all m;

A333230.name: Positions of weak ascents in the sequence of differences between primes.
A333230.formula: Numbers k such that prime(k+2) - 2*prime(k+1) + prime(k) >= 0.

A333231.name: Positions of weak descents in the sequence of differences between primes.
A333231.formula: Numbers k such that prime(k+2) - 2*prime(k+1) + prime(k) >= 0.

A335277.name: First index of strictly increasing prime quartets.

A339378.name: Let n be a positive integer. For each prime divisor p of n, consider the highest power of p which does not exceed n. The sum a(n) of these powers is defined as the power-sum of n.

A339465.name: Primes p such that (p-1)/gpf(p-1) = 2^q * 3^r with q, r >= 1, where gpf(m) is the greatest prime factor of m, A006530.

A343101.name: Pairs of integers (k, m) ordered by m with 1 < k < m such that k has the same prime divisors as m, and, k+1 has the same prime divisors as m+1.

A343507.name: a(n) is the smallest nonnegative integer k such that (2*k)! / (k+n)!^2 is an integer.

A344005.name: a(n) = smallest positive m such that n divides the oblong number m*(m+1).

A352287.name: Numbers k such that, for every prime p dividing k, k has a nontrivial divisor which is congruent to 1 (mod p).

A357513.name: a(n) = numerator of Sum_{k = 1..n} (1/k^3) * binomial(n,k)^2 * binomial(n+k,k)^2 for n >= 1 with a(0) = 0.
A357513.formula: Conjecture: a(p-1) == 0 (mod p^4) for all primes p >= 3 except p = 7 (checked up to p = 499).

A358684.name: a(n) is the minimum integer k such that the smallest prime factor of the n-th Fermat number exceeds 2^(2^n - k).

A359747.name: Numbers k such that k*(k+1) has in its canonical prime factorization mutually distinct exponents.

A360659.name: a(n) is the minimum sum of a completely multiplicative sign sequence of length n.

A362137.name: Smallest size of an n-paradoxical tournament built as a directed Paley graph.

A363069.name: Size of the largest subset of {1,2,...,n} such that no two elements sum to a perfect square.
A363069.formula: The set: {k | k <= n, k == 1 (mod 3)} provides a lower bound: a(n) >= floor((n+2)/3).

A364132.name: a(n) is the smallest positive integer such that from the set {1, 2, ..., a(n)} one can choose an increasing sequence (s(1), s(2), ..., s(n)) in which every segment has a unique sum of elements.

A364153.name: a(n) is the smallest positive integer such that from the set {1, 2, ..., a(n)} one can choose a sequence (s(1), s(2), ..., s(n)) in which every segment has a unique sum.

A365339.name: Length of the longest subsequence of 1,...,n on which the Euler totient function phi A000010 is nondecreasing.
A365339.formula: Tao proves that a(n) ~ n/log n. a(n) >= pi(n) + 64 for all n >= 31957; Pollack, Pomerance, & Treviño conjecture that this is an equality. - _Charles R Greathouse IV_, Dec 08 2023

A367090.name: Numbers that cannot be written as a sum of distinct powers of 3 and distinct powers of 4.
A367090.formula: Complement of A005836 + A000695 = { x+y ; x in A005836 and y in A000695 }.

A371134.name: Decimal expansion of Sum_{squarefree k>=1} k / 2^k.

A372040.name: Smallest k such that there is an n-element subset of {1, 2, ..., k} that does not contain a (nonempty) subset that sums to a square.
A372040.formula: n^3/log^k n << a(n) << n^3 for some constant k.

A372306.name: Cardinality of the largest subset of {1,...,n} such that no three distinct elements of this subset multiply to a square.

A373114.name: Cardinality of the largest subset of {1,...,n} such that no odd number of terms from this subset multiply to a square.
A373114.formula: n-2*a(n) = A360659(n) (see Footnote 2 of the linked paper of Tao).

A373178.name: Cardinality of the largest subset of {1,...,n} such that no five distinct elements of this subset multiply to a square.

A373319.name: Denominator of the asymptotic density of numbers that are unitarily divided by n.
A373319.formula: a(n) = n^2 if and only if n is a cyclic number (A003277).

A375071.name: Smallest k such that Product_{i=1..k} (n+i) divides Product_{i=1..k} (n+k+i), or 0 if there is no such k.

A375077.name: Smallest k such that Product_{i=0..n} (k-i) divides C(2*k,k).

A375081.name: Smallest k>n such that the denominator of Sum {i=n..k} (1/i) is larger than the denominator of Sum {i=n..k+1} (1/i).
A375081.formula: a(n) < 4.374*n for all n > 1. - _Wouter van Doorn_, Nov 06 2024

A377587.name: a(n) is the smallest odd integer m with m-2^k not squarefree for all 1<=k<=n.

A380791.name: For a positive rational x, let k(x) be the smallest positive integer such that all k >= k(x) have a partition into distinct parts with reciprocal sum equal to x. The n-th term in this sequence is equa

A381776.name: Empty polygon numbers: a(n) is the smallest number of points in the plane (with no three of them collinear) such that an empty convex n-gon cannot be avoided.

A382395.name: Number of maximum sized subsets of {1..n} such that every pair of distinct elements has a different difference.

A382397.name: Minimum size of a maximal subset of {1..n} such that every pair of distinct elements has a different difference.

A383044.name: Numbers m such that phi(m) + phi(m+phi(m)) = m where phi is the Euler totient function.

A384927.name: a(n) is the maximum size of a subset S of {1, 2, ..., n} such that for any distinct elements t, u in S, t + u does not divide t*u.
A384927.formula: a(n) = a(n-1) + 1 for n in A174905. - _Elijah Beregovsky_, Nov 19 2025

A385316.name: Smallest number that is the sum of 3 cubes of primes in exactly n different ways.

A385657.name: Number of nonisomorphic maximally dense unit-distance graphs on n vertices.

A386439.name: Decimal expansion of the maximal density of a set of positive integers free of subsets of the form {n, 2n, 3n}.

A386620.name: a(n) is the smallest integer k > 2*n such that Product_{i=1..n} (k - i) has no prime factor p in n < p < 2*n.

A386893.name: Minimal number of Farey fractions in between two fractions that are not similarly ordered.

A386978.name: Numbers k such that the k-th prime gap contains an integer whose least prime factor is greater than or equal to the length of the prime gap.

A387053.name: a(n) is the least k such that n is the sum of a prime and k powers of 2.

A387054.name: Elements of A388654 that do not lie in A070003.

A387184.name: Numbers d such that a!*b!*c!*d! is a perfect square for some 1<=a<b<c<d.

A387502.name: Number of debut sums of initial subsequences of the divisors > 1 of n.

A387503.name: Total number of distinct sums of initial subsequences of the divisors > 1 of positive integers up to n.

A387543.name: a(n) is the size of the largest subset of {1, 2, ..., n} containing n in which any two numbers share a prime factor.

A387584.name: Size of largest subset of {1, ..., n} such that any 4 elements (a <= b <= c <= d) whose product is square implies a*d = b*c.

A387688.name: Number of solutions to n = 2^r + 3^s + 2^t * 3^u where r, s, t and u are nonnegative integers.

A387698.name: Irregular triangle read by rows in which row n is the maximum clique containing n in the gcd-graph of {1, 2, ..., n}. If more than one such clique exists, choose the lexicographically earliest.
A387698.formula: The length of row n is A387543(n).

A387704.name: Size of the maximal subset S of {1,2,...,n} such that for all a, b, c in S not necessarily distinct, a+b+c is unique up to permutation.

A387858.name: Sum of squares of the multiplicities of pairwise distances among the vertices of a regular n-gon.
A387858.formula: a(n) = n^2*(n-1)/2 for odd n; a(n) = n^2*(2*n-3)/4 for even n.

A387864.name: Numbers r for which there are at least two integers strictly between prime(r) and prime(r+1), all of whose prime factors are less than prime(r+1) - prime(r).

A387897.name: Decimal expansion of the connective constant of the square lattice.

A388302.name: a(n) = is the smallest m >= 1 such that n! = b_1*...*b_t with b_1 < ... < b_t and m = b_t - b_1.

A388654.name: Elements of an interval of natural numbers whose product is divisible by the square of the largest prime factor.

A388850.name: Initial term of first maximal bad interval of width n, i.e., initial term of the first run of exactly n+1 consecutive integers in A388654; or 0 if no such interval exists.

A388851.name: Numbers c such that a! * b! * c! is a perfect square for some 1 <= a < b < c.

A389100.name: Elements of a non-singleton interval of natural numbers whose product is divisible by the square of the largest prime factor of the product.

A389117.name: Decimal expansion of the sum of the distinct entries of 1/A055204(n)^(1/2).

A389148.name: Composite numbers e>6 for which there is no 1<=a<b<c<d<e for which a!b!c!d!e! is a perfect square.

A389182.name: Maximum cardinality of a subset of {1,...,n} in which all sums a+b of elements a<=b are distinct except possibly one.

A389241.name: Maximum number of distinct consecutive sums of a permutation of [n].

A389244.name: Powerful part of n*(n+1): a(n) = A057521(n*(n+1)).

A389313.name: a(n) is the smallest m such that for every red-blue edge-coloring of the graph K_{m} there exists either a red or a blue n-cycle; Ramsey number r(C_n, C_n).

A389335.name: a(n) is the smallest m such that for every red-green-blue edge-coloring of the graph K_{m} there exists at least one red, green or blue n-cycle; Ramsey number r(C_n, C_n, C_n).
A389335.formula: A conjecture by Bondy and Erdős: a(n) <= 4n - 3 for n >= 4.

A389360.name: Smallest m >= 2*n such that binomial(m,n) is a multiple of m-i for all 0<=i<n, but one.

A389396.name: Numbers k such that binomial(2*k,k) is divisible by (k+1)^2*(k+2)^2.

A389479.name: a(n) is the Frobenius number for the set { binomial(m,k), k=1..m-1 } where m = A024619(n).
A389479.formula: If m = Product_{k=1..c} p_k^b_k, then a(n) = -m + Sum_{k=1..c} Sum_{d=1..b_k} binomial(m,p_k^d)*(p_k-1) where m=A024619(n).

A389544.name: a(n) is the smallest integer greater than a(n-1) such that all consecutive products in a(1)..a(n) are distinct, with a(1) = 2.

A389646.name: Maximum number of edges that need to be removed from a triangle-free graph on n vertices to make it bipartite.

A389676.name: a(n) = Min_{0<i<n} (prime(n-i) + prime(n+i)).
A389676.formula: A conjecture of Pomerance: lim sup (a(n)-2*prime(n)) = oo.

A389677.name: a(n) = A389676(n) - 2*prime(n).
A389677.formula: A conjecture of Pomerance: lim sup (a(n)) = oo.

A389680.name: Numbers k for which no i > 1 exists such that k + i is composite and its least prime factor exceeds i^2.

A389713.name: a(1) = 3, a(2) = 5, a(n) is the smallest prime such that a(n) - a(n-1) + 1 is in the sequence.

A389784.name: a(n) is the maximum size of a subset A of {1,...,n} such that no element in A is the average of a subset of A with cardinality at least 2.

A389839.name: Smallest even number which cannot be written as the difference of two consecutive numbers which are relatively prime to the primorial prime(n)#.

A389975.name: Maximum cardinality of a set of disjoint congruence classes with distinct moduli, each at most n.

A390181.name: Numbers not able to be represented in the form c*p^2+b for p prime, c >= 1 and 0 <= b < p.

A390187.name: Minimum number of distinct consecutive sums of a permutation of [n].

A390246.name: a(n) is the least integer k such that there exist n distinct integers b_1, ..., b_n with n < b_i < n+k and b_i is divisible by i for 1 <= i <= n.

A390256.name: Minimum size of maximum clique or independent set of a graph on n vertices.

A390257.name: Minimum size of maximum regular induced subgraph of a graph on n vertices.
A390257.formula: a(n) = max{ k | A394563(k) <= n }. - _Brendan McKay_, Apr 11 2026

A390380.name: Integers k which cannot be written in the form x^2 + y^2 - z^2, where x, y, z are integers and x^2, y^2, z^2 <= k.

A390393.name: a(n) is the maximum size of a subset A of {1,...,n} such that Sum_{k in S} 1/k != 1 for all subsets S of A.

A390394.name: a(n) is the maximum size of a subset A of {1,...,n} such that there are no solutions to 1/a = 1/b_1 + ... + 1/b_k with distinct a, b_1, ..., b_k in A.

A390395.name: a(n) is the maximum size of a subset S of {1,...,n} such that there are no solutions to 1/a = 1/b + 1/c for distinct a,b,c in S.

A390642.name: a(n) is the smallest integer k such that the number of sums a(i) + a(j) <= k for i <= j < n is less than k - n + 1.

A390645.name: Triangle read by rows: T(n,r) is maximal such that there exists a family F of subsets of {1,...,n} of size T(n,r) such that the intersection of no two sets in F has r elements.

A390769.name: Least even positive integer k that does not appear in the first n prime gaps.

A390813.name: a(n) is the size of the largest Sidon subset of the first n positive perfect squares.

A390919.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 5 or greater.

A391118.name: Let B_n = {b_1 < b_2 < ...} be the set of those integers in [n, n^2] which have a divisor in (n, 2n). a(n) = max(b_(i+1) - b_i).

A391431.name: Numerator of k + a/k where n = k^2 + 2*a + 1 and -k <= a <= k.

A391490.name: Denominator of k + a/k where n = k^2 + 2a + 1 and -k <= a <= k.

A391592.name: Maximum size of a subset S of {1..n} such that all subset sums of {1/k : k in S} are distinct.

A391599.name: Minimum size of an intersecting family of n-sets such that every set of size at most n-1 is disjoint from at least one member of the family.
A391599.formula: (8/3)*n - O(1) <= a(n) <= C*n for some constant C and all sufficiently large n.

A391668.name: Table read by antidiagonals. T(n,k) is the least number coprime to all numbers in [n+1, n+k].

A391750.name: Maximum length of an increasing sequence, bounded by n, in which the largest prime divisors of the elements form a decreasing sequence.

A392019.name: If binomial(n,k) = u*v where the only primes dividing u are <= k and the only primes dividing v are > k, then a(n) is the least k such that u > n^2, or 0 if such a k doesn't exist.

A392164.name: a(n) is the size of the largest subset S of {1,...,N} such that every element of S+S is squarefree.

A392165.name: Indices of record values in A392164.

A392342.name: Numbers that are not the sum of at most four cubefull numbers.

A392343.name: Numbers that are not the sum of at most five 4-full numbers.

A392636.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 6 or greater.

A393168.name: Integers k which can be written in the form x^2 + y^2 - z^2, where x, y, z are integers and x^2, y^2, z^2 <= k.

A393584.name: a(n) is the minimum, over all partitions {X, Y} of {1..2n} with |X| = |Y|, of the maximum number of pairs (x, y) with the same difference x - y for x in X and y in Y.

A394400.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 4 or greater.

A394462.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 6.

A394539.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 5.

A394563.name: Least integer a(n) such that every graph on a(n) vertices has an induced regular subgraph of order at least n.

A394564.name: Least integer a(n) such that every graph on a(n) vertices has an induced regular subgraph of order n.

A394573.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 4.

A394574.name: a(n) is the greatest k such that every graph on n vertices has an induced regular subgraph of order k.

A394930.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 7.

A394933.name: Number of graphs with n vertices that have no induced regular subgraph of order 7 or greater.
